name: Galibi Carib

ancestors.notes: Descent per Glottolog classification of Galibi Carib (gali1262).

notes: Cariban language (Kariña/Galibi) of Venezuela and the Guianas, Latin orthography. The grapheme ï denotes the high central vowel /ɨ/. The voiced stops b/d/g overlap with post-nasal allophones of the voiceless stops p/t/k and are treated by some descriptions as conditioned rather than independent; they are mapped here at face value. Stress, palatalization allophones ([tʃ ʃ ɲ]) and fricative allophones ([β]) that the spelling does not distinguish are left unencoded; there is no tone.

sources.author: Hammarström, H. et al. (Glottolog 5.0)
sources.title: Glottolog: Galibi Carib (gali1262)
sources.publisher: Max Planck Institute for Evolutionary Anthropology
